report_subtitle: Safety performance and actionable findings from facility-wide audit
executive_summary: The audit identified three key areas: forklift near-misses in the loading bay during shift changes, late fire-exit documentation in June (30%), and strong PPE compliance at 94%. Actionable operational changes can address the first two; the PPE program should continue unchanged.
findings_note: Summary of facility-wide safety performance and risk areas.
recommendations: Stagger shift-change times to reduce congestion and near-miss incidents in the loading bay.
Relocate fire-exit checks to morning walk routines to ensure timely documentation.
Maintain current PPE compliance program; performance above target demonstrates program effectiveness.
conclusion_statement: Shift-change staggering and morning exit checks will address the identified risks while sustaining strong PPE performance.
next_steps: Implement shift-change stagger protocol — owner TBC
Revise exit-check documentation schedule to morning walk — owner TBC
Review PPE program metrics quarterly — owner TBC

finding_slides.finding_heading: Forklift near-misses concentrated in loading bay during shift changes
finding_points: Incident clustering observed during the 2–4 PM shift transitions.
Root cause: increased foot and vehicle traffic overlap during handoff periods.
Hazard: inadequate physical separation during peak congestion times.

finding_slides.finding_heading: 30% of fire-exit checks logged late in June
finding_points: Late documentation identified for exit checks in June; timing inconsistent.
Current check process occurs mid-shift, competing with operational demands.
Risk: delayed response to potential exit blockages or signage failures.
Strong sustained compliance rate demonstrates effective safety culture.
Equipment availability and employee training reinforced this performance.
No action required; program warrants continued support and resourcing.
